summary: Stellar soundboard capture of the March 25 1990 Knickerbocker Arena show, highlighted by a monster “Eyes” opener and magical Bird Song.
review: The performance is widely praised for its inspired second‑set—especially the free‑form ‘Eyes’ opening, soaring ‘Bird Song,’ and tight renditions of ‘Samson & Delilah,’ ‘Let It Grow,’ and ‘Black Peter.’ The Healy soundboard source delivers crystal‑clear audio with just a touch of excessive cathedral reverb, making this one of the finest 1990 recordings.

ai_rating.rationale: Nearly all reviewers give 5‑star ratings, praising both musical performance and soundboard quality; only minor complaints (reverb excess, a couple of lyric slips) keep the score just shy of perfect.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Healy SBD is described as “best recording I've heard”; crisp and balanced overall, though one reviewer notes an over‑done cathedral reverb that makes it a bit dizzy.

show_quality.setlist_flow: Rare opening with Greatest Story Ever Told → Touch of Grey sets an exciting tone; second set flows superbly from the monster Eyes opener through Samson, Bird Song and into a tight, mostly flawless sequence.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Garcia’s guitar is described as “on fire” and his solos magical (Bird Song, Eyes); only minor vocal slip‑ups noted in the Eyes performance.
band_member_comments.Phil: One reviewer wonders if Phil ordered the heavy cathedral reverb on the board; no direct comments on his bass playing.
band_member_comments.Bob: General praise that “the boys nailed every note,” implying solid rhythm guitar work.
band_member_comments.Keys: Brent Mydland gets special kudos for vocal highlights on ‘Never Trust a Woman’ and ‘I Will Take You Home’; keyboards are praised as part of the overall tight band sound.
band_member_comments.Drums: Mickey & Bill receive mixed notes – a brief “unspectacular Drums” comment, but also credit for taking over after Truckin' and providing space.
